title: Give shared mutable state a single synchronization owner
description: Confine mutable state behind one lock or one owning task so every access goes through the same guard.
body: Use when a value is read and written from more than one thread or task. Do not sprinkle locks ad hoc or assume a single write is atomic because it looks small. Actions: identify the shared variable, pick one synchronization mechanism (a mutex it lives behind, an owning actor/task, or an atomic), route every read and write through it, and remove direct accesses. Validation signals include a race detector running clean over the paths and every access site holding the same lock or going through the owner. Risk boundary: removing the guard on shared mutable state because 'it is probably fine' is a correctness regression; require a detector run before accepting it.
